diagnostics.title: Build pipeline runs code near secrets
diagnostics.message: The build or install pipeline can execute package lifecycle code while CI secrets may be present.
diagnostics.help: Run dependency installs with scripts disabled before exposing secrets, isolate untrusted build code, and move signing/deploy authority into a narrow privileged step.

diagnostics.title: Plugin or updater trust boundary risk
diagnostics.message: Code appears to download, install, update, or execute plugin/updater content across a trust boundary.
diagnostics.help: Require signed updates/plugins, pin trusted repositories, verify hashes before execution, and keep custom repository installs behind explicit warnings.

diagnostics.title: Build pipeline runs code near secrets
diagnostics.message: The build or install pipeline can execute package lifecycle code while CI secrets may be present.
diagnostics.help: Run dependency installs with scripts disabled before exposing secrets, isolate untrusted build code, and move signing/deploy authority into a narrow privileged step.

diagnostics.title: await inside a loop
diagnostics.message: This makes the for…of loop slow because each await runs one after another, so collect the independent calls & run them together with `await Promise.all(items.map(...))`
diagnostics.help: Collect the items, then use `await Promise.all(items.map(...))` so independent work runs at the same time

diagnostics.title: Non-inlinable process.env access (Expo)
diagnostics.message: Computed `process.env[...]` access isn't inlined by babel-preset-expo and is `undefined` at runtime. Use static `process.env.EXPO_PUBLIC_NAME`.
diagnostics.help: Read env vars with static dotted access (`process.env.EXPO_PUBLIC_NAME`). Computed access and destructuring aren't inlined by babel-preset-expo and resolve to `undefined` at runtime.

diagnostics.title: React 19 API migration can break callers
diagnostics.message: forwardRef is dead weight in React 19, since ref is a normal prop now, so drop it & pass ref straight through.
diagnostics.help: Pass `ref` as a normal prop on function components, since `forwardRef` isn't needed in React 19. Replace `useContext(X)` with `use(X)`. Only runs on React 19+ projects.

diagnostics.title: State initializer runs on every render
diagnostics.message: useState(getPreferences()) re-runs getPreferences() on every render & throws the result away.
diagnostics.help: Wrap expensive initial state in an arrow function so the initializer does not rerun and get thrown away on every render.

diagnostics.title: Children type checked at runtime
diagnostics.message: Your users hit inconsistent behavior because `typeof children === "string"` makes this component switch on what callers pass, so add clear subcomponents like `<Button.Text>` instead.
diagnostics.help: Add clear subcomponents like `<Button.Text>` and `<Button.Icon>` so callers don't have to check `typeof children`.

diagnostics.title: Effect re-subscribes on a changing callback
diagnostics.message: Your effect re-subscribes whenever "onComplete" changes, even though it's only used inside `setTimeout`.
diagnostics.help: Wrap the callback with `useEffectEvent(callback)` (React 19+) and call it inside the sub-handler. An Effect Event always sees the latest props and state but isn't a dependency, so the effect won't re-subscribe every time the parent redraws. See https://react.dev/reference/react/useEffectEvent

diagnostics.help: Run the side effect in the event handler that triggers it, instead of watching its state from a useEffect. See https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect#sharing-logic-between-event-handlers

diagnostics.title: Intl formatter rebuilt each call
diagnostics.message: This is slow because new Intl.DateTimeFormat() rebuilds on every call inside a function, so move it to the top of the file, or wrap it in useMemo
diagnostics.help: Move `new Intl.NumberFormat(...)` to the top of the file or wrap it in `useMemo`. Building one is slow, so don't redo it on every call

diagnostics.title: Multiple components in one file
diagnostics.message: This file declares several components, so each component is harder to find, test, and change.
diagnostics.help: Move secondary components into their own files so each component stays easier to find, test, and change.

diagnostics.title: Sequential independent awaits
diagnostics.message: This await doesn't use the previous result, so your users wait twice as long for nothing.
diagnostics.help: These two awaits don't depend on each other. Wrap them in `Promise.all([...])` so they run at the same time.

diagnostics.title: Independent awaits run sequentially
diagnostics.message: These 3 sequential await statements run one after another even though they look independent, so the page waits longer than it needs to. Run them together with Promise.all() instead
diagnostics.help: Use `const [a, b] = await Promise.all([fetchA(), fetchB()])` so independent calls run at the same time

diagnostics.title: Mutation without cache invalidation
diagnostics.message: useMutation with no cache update leaves your users looking at stale data after it runs.
diagnostics.help: Add `onSuccess: () => queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['...'] })` so cached data stays in sync after the mutation

diagnostics.title: Mutation without cache invalidation
diagnostics.message: useMutation with no cache update leaves your users looking at stale data after it runs.
diagnostics.help: Add `onSuccess: () => queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['...'] })` so cached data stays in sync after the mutation

diagnostics.title: await inside a loop
diagnostics.message: This makes the for…of loop slow because each await runs one after another, so collect the independent calls & run them together with `await Promise.all(items.map(...))`
diagnostics.help: Collect the items, then use `await Promise.all(items.map(...))` so independent work runs at the same time

diagnostics.title: Non-component export in component file
diagnostics.message: This file exports non-components, so Fast Refresh can't safely preserve component state.
diagnostics.help: Move non-component exports out of component files so Fast Refresh can preserve component state instead of full-reloading.

diagnostics.title: Event logic handled in an effect
diagnostics.message: Faking an event handler with a prop plus a useEffect costs an extra render & runs late.

diagnostics.title: Data passed to parent via effect
diagnostics.message: Handing data back to a parent from a useEffect costs your users an extra render.
diagnostics.help: Fetch the data in the parent and pass it down as a prop (or return it from the hook), instead of handing it back up through a prop callback in a useEffect. See https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect#passing-data-to-the-parent

diagnostics.title: Parent kept in sync with a callback effect
diagnostics.message: Your parent re-renders on every local state change because this useEffect calls the prop "onValidationChange" just to stay in sync.
diagnostics.help: Move the shared state into a Provider so both sides read the same value. Then you don't need a useEffect to keep them in sync.

diagnostics.title: Parent kept in sync with a callback effect

diagnostics.help: Split boolean-heavy APIs into smaller components or named variants so combinations stay testable.

diagnostics.title: Dynamic padding on contentContainerStyle
diagnostics.message: Your users see rows jump when a changing paddingBottom on contentContainerStyle shifts the whole list.
diagnostics.help: Use `contentInset={{ bottom: dynamicValue }}` so the OS shifts the content instead of relaying it out, which avoids the jump.

diagnostics.title: Time or random value in JSX
diagnostics.message: This can cause a hydration mismatch because new Date() reached from JSX gives a different value on the server than in the browser. Move it into useEffect+useState to run only in the browser, or add suppressHydrationWarning to the parent if it's on purpose.
diagnostics.help: Move time or random values into useEffect+useState so they only run in the browser, or add suppressHydrationWarning to the parent if it's intentional

diagnostics.title: Touchable components instead of Pressable
diagnostics.message: Your users miss <Pressable>'s flexible press feedback when you use TouchableOpacity, which is old & frozen.
diagnostics.help: Use `<Pressable>` because Touchable* components are frozen and lack Pressable's state-based feedback and accessibility behavior.

diagnostics.title: Boolean prop combinations are hard to test

diagnostics.title: react-native Image instead of expo-image

diagnostics.help: Use `<Image>` from `expo-image` instead of `react-native`. Same props, plus caching, placeholders, and crossfades for faster image loading.

diagnostics.title: Effect re-subscribes on a changing callback
diagnostics.message: Your effect re-subscribes whenever "handleSquadCompletion" changes, even though it's only used inside `subscribe`.
diagnostics.help: Wrap the callback with `useEffectEvent(callback)` (React 19+) and call it inside the sub-handler. An Effect Event always sees the latest props and state but isn't a dependency, so the effect won't re-subscribe every time the parent redraws. See https://react.dev/reference/react/useEffectEvent

diagnostics.help: Use useRef instead of useState when the value is only set and never shown on screen. `ref.current = ...` updates it without redrawing the component.

diagnostics.title: await inside a loop
diagnostics.message: This makes the for-loop slow because each await runs one after another, so collect the independent calls & run them together with `await Promise.all(items.map(...))`
diagnostics.help: Collect the items, then use `await Promise.all(items.map(...))` so independent work runs at the same time

diagnostics.title: Independent awaits run sequentially
diagnostics.message: These 3 sequential await statements run one after another even though they look independent, so the page waits longer than it needs to. Run them together with Promise.all() instead
diagnostics.help: Use `const [a, b] = await Promise.all([fetchA(), fetchB()])` so independent calls run at the same time

diagnostics.title: Dimensions.get over useWindowDimensions
diagnostics.message: Your users see a stale layout on rotation or resize because Dimensions.get() does not update.
diagnostics.help: Use `const { width, height } = useWindowDimensions()` so the size updates automatically on rotation and resize.

diagnostics.title: Non-native JS navigator
diagnostics.message: Users get JS-driven transitions and gestures from @react-navigation/bottom-tabs, instead of platform-native navigation behavior.
diagnostics.help: Use `@react-navigation/native-stack` (or `native-tabs` in v7+) for real native transitions and gestures.

diagnostics.title: React 19 API migration can break callers
diagnostics.message: useContext is replaced by `use()` in React 19, which reads context inside ifs & loops too, so switch to `import { use } from 'react'`.
diagnostics.help: Pass `ref` as a normal prop on function components, since `forwardRef` isn't needed in React 19. Replace `useContext(X)` with `use(X)`. Only runs on React 19+ projects.

diagnostics.title: Event logic handled in an effect
diagnostics.message: Faking an event handler with state plus a useEffect costs an extra render & runs late.
diagnostics.help: Run the side effect in the event handler that triggers it, instead of watching its state from a useEffect. See https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect#sharing-logic-between-event-handlers

diagnostics.title: Event logic handled in an effect
diagnostics.message: Faking an event handler with a prop plus a useEffect costs an extra render & runs late.
diagnostics.help: Run the side effect in the event handler that triggers it, instead of watching its state from a useEffect. See https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect#sharing-logic-between-event-handlers

diagnostics.title: Build pipeline runs code near secrets
diagnostics.message: The build or install pipeline can execute package lifecycle code while CI secrets may be present.
diagnostics.help: Run dependency installs with scripts disabled before exposing secrets, isolate untrusted build code, and move signing/deploy authority into a narrow privileged step.

diagnostics.title: Plugin or updater trust boundary risk
diagnostics.message: Code appears to download, install, update, or execute plugin/updater content across a trust boundary.
diagnostics.help: Require signed updates/plugins, pin trusted repositories, verify hashes before execution, and keep custom repository installs behind explicit warnings.

diagnostics.title: React 19 API migration can break callers
diagnostics.message: forwardRef is dead weight in React 19, since ref is a normal prop now, so drop it & pass ref straight through.
diagnostics.help: Pass `ref` as a normal prop on function components, since `forwardRef` isn't needed in React 19. Replace `useContext(X)` with `use(X)`. Only runs on React 19+ projects.

diagnostics.title: Single-element style array adds wasted allocation
diagnostics.message: Your users pay for an extra array allocation when "style" wraps a single value for nothing.
diagnostics.help: Use `style={value}` instead of `style={[value]}`. A one-item array just adds extra work for nothing.

diagnostics.help: Use `<Image>` from `expo-image` instead of `react-native`. Same props, plus caching, placeholders, and crossfades for faster image loading.

diagnostics.title: Live state pushed to parent via effect

diagnostics.help: Move the state up to the parent (or return it from the hook), instead of handing it back up through a prop callback in a useEffect. See https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect#notifying-parent-components-about-state-changes

diagnostics.title: Non-component export in component file

diagnostics.help: Move non-component exports out of component files so Fast Refresh can preserve component state instead of full-reloading.

diagnostics.title: Inline renderItem on list
diagnostics.message: Your users see extra row work when renderItem on <FlashList> is rebuilt every time the screen redraws.
diagnostics.help: Move renderItem to a named function or wrap it in useCallback so it is not rebuilt every time the screen redraws.

diagnostics.title: Inline handler in list renderItem
diagnostics.message: This onPress is rebuilt for every row, so your memo() rows still redraw.
diagnostics.help: Move the handler out with useCallback at list scope and pass the row id as a prop. Then memo() rows skip redrawing when their data has not changed.

diagnostics.message: This object is rebuilt for every row, so your memo() rows still redraw.

diagnostics.help: Collect the items, then use `await Promise.all(items.map(...))` so independent work runs at the same time

diagnostics.title: flex on contentContainerStyle collapses container
diagnostics.message: `flex` on contentContainerStyle can collapse the container on small screens.
diagnostics.help: In RN, `flex: 1` on `contentContainerStyle` sets `flexBasis: 0` and can collapse the container on small screens. Use `flexGrow: 1` instead.

diagnostics.title: Non-virtualized mapped list in ScrollView
diagnostics.message: Your users get slow scrolling when <ScrollView> with items.map(...) builds every row at once.
diagnostics.help: `<ScrollView>{items.map(...)}</ScrollView>` builds every row at once, which slows scrolling. Use FlashList, LegendList, or FlatList instead.

diagnostics.title: Non-virtualized mapped list in ScrollView
diagnostics.message: Your users get slow scrolling when <ScrollView> with items.map(...) builds every row at once.
diagnostics.help: `<ScrollView>{items.map(...)}</ScrollView>` builds every row at once, which slows scrolling. Use FlashList, LegendList, or FlatList instead.

diagnostics.title: Independent awaits run sequentially
diagnostics.message: These 4 sequential await statements run one after another even though they look independent, so the page waits longer than it needs to. Run them together with Promise.all() instead
diagnostics.help: Use `const [a, b] = await Promise.all([fetchA(), fetchB()])` so independent calls run at the same time

diagnostics.title: await before an early-return guard
diagnostics.message: This await blocks the function before an early-return that doesn't use the awaited value, so the skip path waits for nothing. Move the await below the guard so it only runs when you need the data
diagnostics.help: Move the `await` below the early-return guard so the skip path stays fast and avoids unnecessary async work.

diagnostics.title: Large inline style object rebuilds every render
diagnostics.message: This inline style has 8 properties, which is hard to read & rebuilds every render. Move it to a CSS class, CSS module, or styled component.
diagnostics.help: Move the styles to a CSS class, CSS module, Tailwind utilities, or a styled component. Big inline objects are hard to read and rebuild on every update.

diagnostics.message: useContext is replaced by `use()` in React 19, which reads context inside ifs & loops too, so switch to `import { use } from 'react'`.
diagnostics.help: Pass `ref` as a normal prop on function components, since `forwardRef` isn't needed in React 19. Replace `useContext(X)` with `use(X)`. Only runs on React 19+ projects.

diagnostics.title: Unstable context provider value
diagnostics.message: Every reader of this context redraws on each render because you build its `value` inline.
diagnostics.help: Wrap the context value in `useMemo` or move it outside the component so consumers do not redraw every render.

diagnostics.title: react-native Image instead of expo-image
diagnostics.message: Your users watch images reload often because ImageBackground from react-native has no caching.
diagnostics.help: Use `<Image>` from `expo-image` instead of `react-native`. Same props, plus caching, placeholders, and crossfades for faster image loading.

diagnostics.title: Large inline style object rebuilds every render
diagnostics.message: This inline style has 8 properties, which is hard to read & rebuilds every render. Move it to a CSS class, CSS module, or styled component.
diagnostics.help: Move the styles to a CSS class, CSS module, Tailwind utilities, or a styled component. Big inline objects are hard to read and rebuild on every update.

diagnostics.title: Event logic handled in an effect
diagnostics.message: Faking an event handler with a prop plus a useEffect costs an extra render & runs late.
diagnostics.help: Run the side effect in the event handler that triggers it, instead of watching its state from a useEffect. See https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect#sharing-logic-between-event-handlers

diagnostics.title: Effect re-subscribes on a changing callback
diagnostics.message: Your effect re-subscribes whenever "onReappear" changes, even though it's only used inside `setTimeout`.
diagnostics.help: Wrap the callback with `useEffectEvent(callback)` (React 19+) and call it inside the sub-handler. An Effect Event always sees the latest props and state but isn't a dependency, so the effect won't re-subscribe every time the parent redraws. See https://react.dev/reference/react/useEffectEvent

diagnostics.title: State only used in handlers
diagnostics.message: Each update to "dismissTimer" redraws your component for nothing because this useState is set but never shown on screen.
diagnostics.help: Use useRef instead of useState when the value is only set and never shown on screen. `ref.current = ...` updates it without redrawing the component.

diagnostics.title: await inside a loop
diagnostics.message: This makes the for-loop slow because each await runs one after another, so collect the independent calls & run them together with `await Promise.all(items.map(...))`
diagnostics.help: Collect the items, then use `await Promise.all(items.map(...))` so independent work runs at the same time

diagnostics.title: Independent awaits run sequentially
diagnostics.message: These 3 sequential await statements run one after another even though they look independent, so the page waits longer than it needs to. Run them together with Promise.all() instead
diagnostics.help: Use `const [a, b] = await Promise.all([fetchA(), fetchB()])` so independent calls run at the same time

diagnostics.title: Dimensions.get over useWindowDimensions
diagnostics.message: Your users see a stale layout on rotation or resize because Dimensions.get() does not update.
diagnostics.help: Use `const { width, height } = useWindowDimensions()` so the size updates automatically on rotation and resize.

diagnostics.title: Non-native JS navigator
diagnostics.message: Users get JS-driven transitions and gestures from @react-navigation/bottom-tabs, instead of platform-native navigation behavior.
diagnostics.help: Use `@react-navigation/native-stack` (or `native-tabs` in v7+) for real native transitions and gestures.
